title: VaR/CVaR tail overlay with Kupiec/Christoffersen backtests (B0002 shut-off)
source: GitHub repo scout (4 Haiku explorers) + quant-phd-advisor literature review, 2026-05-31. Conversation: 'search quant trading repositories'.
why: The survival book has a hard variance cap; a backtestable tail tripwire would harden the shut-off (B0002 family) before live. quant-phd-advisor: adopt as the SHUT-OFF overlay, not a second sizer (it overlaps the variance cap). Key subtlety: CVaR/ES is NOT elicitable (Gneiting) so it can't be cleanly coverage-backtested; VaR IS backtestable via Kupiec (unconditional) + Christoffersen (conditional).
value_rationale: VERDICT: Adopt-with-caveats, scoped to B0002 shut-off (NOT a second sizing layer). High value because B0002 gates live deployment.
scope: Add a VaR-exceedance tripwire as the shut-off primitive; validate the VaR leg with Kupiec + Christoffersen tests
CRITICAL: treat CVaR as a budgeting number only (validate via Acerbi-Szekely Test 1/2), NEVER via a coverage test — it is not elicitable
Parametric/Gaussian VaR understates fat gold/crypto tails -> use historical or GARCH-MC
Overlay on survival_book variance cap; do NOT duplicate it
recommendation: Adopt-with-caveats as the B0002 shut-off tripwire. Relates to done B0002 (shut-off criteria) — this is its tail-risk hardening.
out_of_scope: Not a second sizer (the variance cap + B0120 ch.10 sizer already size). CVaR coverage-backtesting (statistically invalid).
